target.title: fix(plugins): guard plugin tool descriptor reads
target.body:
## Summary
- guard plugin tool descriptor reads so malformed `name`, `execute`, `description`, or `parameters` access cannot abort sibling tool resolution
- pin each accepted plugin tool to one stable name before allow/deny policy, manifest filtering, metadata, descriptor cache, and cached execution
- keep descriptor caching on validated required fields while treating optional metadata getters (`label`, `displaySummary`) as best-effort

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts` - passed 2 files / 81 tests on `4c0d9558086`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/tools.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/plugins/tools.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean after review-driven fixes, `overall: patch is correct (0.82)`

Blocked broader proof:
- `blacksmith testbox list --all` failed: not authenticated; requires `blacksmith auth login`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --label next181-check-changed-refresh --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` failed before lease creation: coordinator `POST /v1/runs` and `POST /v1/leases` returned HTTP 401 unauthorized
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --label next181-check-changed-refresh --shell -- "env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed"` failed before remote execution: Blacksmith not authenticated

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: malformed plugin tool descriptor access no longer crashes `resolvePluginTools()` or bypasses tool policy before healthy sibling tools resolve.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw worktree on current `origin/main` `18ecb82034d`, using repo Vitest and lint/format wrappers.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts`; focused `oxfmt`, `oxlint`, and `git diff --check` commands listed above; local autoreview on the final patch.

Evidence after fix: focused plugin resolver/cache tests passed 81 tests. Regressions cover unreadable parameters, parameters that become unreadable before descriptor caching, unreadable descriptions, unreadable/changing/unstable names, descriptor traps, non-configurable stable and unstable names, and optional metadata getters during cache capture. Autoreview initially found two real issues around required `description` and required `parameters`; both were fixed and the final review was clean.

Observed result after fix: bad plugin tools are diagnosed and skipped while valid sibling tools remain available. Accepted tools use one stable name through policy, metadata, descriptor caching, and cached execution. Descriptor cache entries are built from already-validated required fields, while optional metadata getters are omitted when unreadable instead of poisoning the cache.

What was not tested: broad `pnpm check:changed` / remote changed gate, because both available maintainer remote paths are auth-blocked from this shell; no live third-party plugin runtime beyond the focused resolver/cache tests.

## Red proof
Before the fix, the unreadable-parameters regression crashed with the plugin getter error before sibling tools resolved. Autoreview then caught order-dependent behavior for unreadable required `description` and required `parameters` during descriptor caching; both now have explicit regressions.
